text:
the absence of macroscopic lesions of the upper gastrointestinal tract, performing multiple step biopsies (2 per segment) makes it possible to demonstrate on histopathology the presence of epithelioid and giant-cell granuloma in 10 to 20% of cases (40).
The diagnostic criteria for pediatric IBD are similar to those for adults. Recommendations for the diagnosis of pediatric IBD, called the "Porto criteria", were published by ESPGHAN (European Society for Paediatric Gastroenterology and Nutrition) (41).
(Tables 1 and 2)
Table 1: pediatric forms of UC at diagnosis (41)
Presentation
Macroscopic characteristics
Microscopic characteristics
Typical
Continuous disease starting from the rectum
Architectural distortion, basal lymphoplasmacytosis, more severe disease in the distal part, no granulomas
Atypical
1.	 Rectal sparing
No macroscopic disease at the rectum or rectosigmoid
Same as the typical type, especially in the segment
affected above the spared area
2.	 Transient form
Continuous disease starting from the rectum, which may
also show rectal sparing
Biopsies sometimes focal, if
